bullets.text: The US President has warned Iran of severe repercussions if it delays agreeing to a deal, following Tehran's vow of retaliation to any attacks. This comes as three Indian sailors are reported missing after a US alleged strike on a tanker in the Gulf of Oman.

An investigation by Global Witness suggests that leading global brands, including Amazon and Sony, may be using coltan sourced from supply chains controlled by M23 rebels in the Democratic Republic of Congo, who are accused of atrocities.

A man was shot dead in Kenya during protests against a proposed US Ebola quarantine facility, highlighting rising anger regarding US plans in the region. Meanwhile, armed bandits in north-west Nigeria abducted dozens of villagers who were invited to discuss peace talks.
The Commonwealth Bank is being pursued in the High Court by shareholders seeking compensation, alleging the bank failed to inform the market, which inflated their share values.
Victoria is implementing two additional maternal and child health nurse visits for all children, as the state prepares a new program for those no longer supported by the NDIS.

An exclusive report reveals One Nation's financial reports are 'incredibly sloppy', showing over $1 million in missing or worthless assets, which an expert says raises questions about the party's fitness to govern.
US inflation surged to a new three-year high of 4.2% in May, partly due to the energy shock from Middle East tensions.
US tech stocks experienced a significant sell-off on Wall Street, with volatility flaring up ahead of SpaceX's market debut, as geopolitical tensions also lifted oil prices.
Germany is embarking on a €100bn initiative to modernize its railway system, marking a crucial test for Europe's largest economy to reverse years of decline.
